2694.history: HMCS Mulgrave (T/Lt. Ralph Morton Meredith, RCNR) was mined in the English Channel off Le Havre, France on 8 October 1944 and heavily damaged, not repaired. Decommissioned on 6 July 1945. Scrapped in May 1947.

T/Lt. Ralph Morton Meredith, RCNR (11 Oct 1943 - 8 Oct 1944)

T/Lt. Frank Goddard Hutchings, RCNR (23 Mar 1943 - 7 Dec 1943)
T/Lt. Robert Laird Borden Hunter, RCNVR (17 Mar 1944 - 25 Mar 1945)

2699.history: Conversion to armed merchant cruiser completed on 3 August 1940. Paid off to be refitted as Anti-Aircraft "cruiser" on 2 January 1943 and recommissioned as such on 7 June 1943. Decommissioned 10 December 1945. Sold into mercantile service in 1948 and renamed Charlton Sovereign. Resold in 1953 when she became the Italian Lucania. Scrapped. See also this website (offsite link).
commanders: Cdr. Charles Tachereau Beard, RCN (31 Jul 1940 - 7 Oct 1940)
Cdr. Frederick Gordon Hart, RCN (8 Oct 1940 - 21 Jun 1942)
A/Capt. Frank Llewellyn Houghton, RCN (22 Jun 1942 - 31 Dec 1942)
Lt.Cdr. Owen Conor Robertson, RCNR (1 Jan 1943 - 5 Jun 1943)
Cdr. Adrian Mitchell Hope, RCN (7 Jun 1943 - 7 Dec 1944)
Capt. Wallace Bourchier Creery, RCN (8 Dec 1944 - 10 Dec 1945)

2700.history: Conversion to armed merchant cruiser completed on 4 December 1940. Rebuilt as Landing Ship Infantry LSI(M). Refit started on 6 March 1943 and recommissioned as such on 6 January 1944. As LSI(M) she was armed with 2 4" AA guns (1x2), 1 40mm AA, 6 20mm AA (6x1). She carried 2 LCM's, 1 LCS(M) and 5 LCA's or LCP(L)'s and 444 troops. Decommissioned 15 April 1945. Sold to the Ministry of Transport and renamed Empire Parkeston. Scrapped in 1962.
commanders: A/Capt. Ronald Ian Agnew, OBE, RCN (4 Dec 1940 - 19 Dec 1941)
A/Capt. John Crispo Ingles Edwards, RCN (20 Dec 1941 - 31 Dec 1942)
A/Capt. Frank Llewellyn Houghton, RCN (1 Jan 1943 - 18 Mar 1943)
A/Cdr. Edward William Finch-Noyes, RCN (19 Mar 1943 - 22 Mar 1943)
T/Lt.Cdr. Thomas Kenneth Young, RCNR (23 Mar 1943 - 23 May 1943)
T/Lt.Cdr. Thomas Douglas Kelly, RCNR (24 May 1943 - 29 Nov 1943)
Cdr. Kenneth Frederick Adams, RCN (30 Nov 1943 - 11 Dec 1943)
Capt. Valentine Stuart Godfrey, RCN (12 Dec 1943 - 6 Apr 1945)

2701.history: Conversion to armed merchant cruiser completed on 28 December 1940. Rebuilt as Landing Ship Infantry LSI(M) from 1 March 1943 and recommissioned as such on 6 January 1944. As LSI(M) she was armed with 2 4" AA guns (1x2), 1 40mm AA, 6 20mm AA (6x1). She carried 2 LCM's, 1 LCS(M) and 5 LCA's or LCP(L)'s and 444 troops. Decommissioned 11 June 1945. Sold into mercantile service in 1946 as Charlton Monarch. Scrapped at Swansea, Wales in 1951.
commanders: T/Cdr. William Barclay Armit, RCNR (28 Dec 1940 - 24 Mar 1941)
Cdr. Kenneth Frederick Adams, RCN (25 Mar 1941 - 1 Dec 1941)
A/Capt. Valentine Stuart Godfrey, RCN (2 Dec 1941 - 18 Mar 1942)
T/Lt. Thomas Douglas Kelly, RCNR (19 Mar 1942 - 16 Apr 1942)
A/Capt. Valentine Stuart Godfrey, RCN (17 Apr 1942 - 17 Apr 1943)
T/Lt.Cdr. Thomas Douglas Kelly, RCNR (18 Apr 1943 - 11 Jun 1945)

2702.history: HMS Uganda was transferred to the Royal Canadian Navy on 21 October 1944 commissioning as HMCS Uganda on transfer. Renamed Quebec on 14 January 1952. Decommissioned on 15 June 1956. Sold and arrived at Osaka, Japan on 6 February 1961 to be broken up for scrap.
commanders: Capt. Edmond Rollo Mainguy, OBE, RCN (21 Oct 1944 - 4 Jul 1946)

2717.history: Decommissioned and returned to the Royal Navy on 17 June 1945.
commanders: T/Lt. Andrew John Wilkie, RNR (Mar 1942 - Feb 1943)
T/Lt. John Clayton Tytherleigh, RNR (Feb 1943 - Feb 1944)
T/Lt. John Craig Boyd, RNVR (Feb 1944 - mid 1945)

2718.history: Baffin was crewed by sailors from the Royal Navy. She was engaged in minesweeping duties out of Halifax, Nova Scotia. Decommissioned and returned to the Royal Navy on 20 August 1945. In 1947 she was sold for merchantile use under the same name, becoming Niedermehnen in 1952.
commanders: E S Bennett, RNVR (Mar 1942 - 21 Aug 1942)
T/Lt. Geoffrey Holder Jones, RNVR (21 Aug 1942 - late 1943)
T/Lt. Archibald Albert Frederick Hodge, RNVR (1 Dec 1943 - late 1943)

2719.history: Decommissioned by the Royal Canadian Navy on 17 June 1945 and returned to the Royal Navy the same day. Sold in 1946. This ship is laying in Kristiansund, Norway for rebuilding to trawler. The ship was after WWII rebuild as trawler and was active to 1972 and then laid up. Name of the ship is S/T Borgenes.
commanders: T/Lt. Rowland Frank Pretty, RNVR (Aug 1942 - Aug 1944)
T/A/Lt.Cdr. Alexander Michael Sullivan, RNVR (Aug 1944 - )
